factors.summary: The company is launching new uniforms, which is a minor product launch.

evidence.text: We expect to recognize a portion of the grant as contra-operating expense. The increase in other expense is primarily driven by costs resulting from our decision in January 2020 to launch new uniforms for flight attendants and above-wing customer service agents.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a significant reduction in capacity, with load factors and revenue per available seat mile (RASM) declining across all regions. This indicates a substantial negative impact on the factor due to lower demand and operational adjustments in response to the pandemic.

evidence.text: System capacity is expected to be down approximately 85 percent compared to the June 2019 quarter, with international capacity to be reduced by approximately 90 percent and domestic flying to be reduced by approximately 80 percent. TRASM decreased 13.1% compared to the prior year period.
We expect fuel consumption to decline consistent with the capacity reductions we are making in response to the pandemic.
Available seat miles decreased by 5.7%, load factor decreased by 9.6 points, and PRASM decreased by 13.3%. TRASM decreased by 13.1%.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant reduction in capacity and decline in load factors and RASM due to the pandemic.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a significant decline in pricing power due to decreased demand, leading to lower revenue metrics such as TRASM and PRASM. The COVID-19 pandemic has exacerbated this issue with reduced advance bookings and cash flow.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating revenue generated a 13.1% decrease in total revenue per available seat mile ('TRASM') and a 12.3% decrease in TRASM, adjusted compared to the March 2019 quarter.
The current reduction in demand for air travel due to the COVID-19 pandemic has resulted in an unprecedented low level of advance bookings and the associated cash received.
Passenger mile yield decreased by 2.0%, PRASM decreased by 13.3%, and TRASM decreased by 13.1%. These metrics indicate a decline in pricing power.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Decline in pricing power due to decreased demand and pandemic effects.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are experiencing a significant decline across all regions, driven by reduced demand resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic. This decline is evident in decreased passenger revenue, reduced operating statistics such as revenue passenger miles and load factor, and significant flight cancellations leading to refunds and rebookings.

evidence.text: Passenger revenue decreased $1.9 billion, or 18.0%, due to reduced demand resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic.
The decrease in operating revenue generated a 13.1% decrease in TRASM and a 12.3% decrease in TRASM, adjusted compared to the March 2019 quarter.

Among other effects of the COVID-19 pandemic affecting air travel and our business: ... Employers ... have issued instructions to employees to work from home and/or otherwise dissuading or restricting air travel; ... cancellations, the waiver of change fees and other refunds have negatively affected our revenues and liquidity, and we expect such negative effects to continue.

We have experienced significant cancellations beginning in the second half of March, which has led to issuance of refunds to customers, while the remainder have been rebooked on future flights or received credits in lieu of cash refunds.
Revenue passenger miles decreased by 16.6%, available seat miles decreased by 5.7%, and load factor decreased by 9.6 points, all due to reduced demand.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant decline in unit volumes across all regions due to reduced demand from the pandemic.

factors.summary: Demand for air travel is significantly reduced due to the COVID-19 pandemic, with expectations that this decline will continue into the foreseeable future. The reduction in demand is unprecedented and has led to adverse impacts on revenues, financial position, and cash flows. The severity and duration of this downturn remain uncertain, posing significant risks to the company's operations and financial stability.

evidence.text: Demand for travel declined at an accelerated pace, which has had an unprecedented and materially adverse impact on our revenues and financial position. The length and severity of the reduction in demand due to the pandemic is uncertain; accordingly, we expect the adverse impact to grow in the June 2020 quarter.

The rapid spread of COVID-19, as well as the escalating measures governments and private organizations have implemented in order to stem the spread of this pandemic, are having a material adverse effect on the demand for worldwide air travel, and consequently upon our business.
Due to the impact of COVID-19 on our ticket purchases, we expect to experience negative cash flows from operations through the June 2020 quarter and possibly beyond.

As a result of decreased demand for air travel due to the COVID-19 pandemic, we have instituted a hiring freeze, reduced salaries by 50% and 25% for our officer and director level employees, respectively, and reduced work hours by 25% for all other management and most front-line employee work groups for the June 2020 quarter.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The demand for air travel has significantly decreased due to COVID-19, leading to substantial revenue declines and financial instability.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a significant headwind due to the COVID-19 pandemic, negatively impacting business operations, cash flows, and air travel demand.

evidence.text: The rapid spread of the COVID-19 virus and measures implemented to combat it are having, and are likely to continue to have, a material adverse effect on our business.
The impact of COVID-19 on our ticket purchases, resulting in negative cash flows and reduced demand.
The COVID-19 pandemic is causing a significant decrease in demand for air travel, leading to substantial reductions in revenue and operational adjustments.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The COVID-19 pandemic is creating unfavorable economic conditions, impacting business operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: The travel demand mix is shifting unfavorably, primarily driven by a significant decline in international and business travel due to the COVID-19 pandemic. This shift is resulting in lower yields and margins, as leisure travel, though present, does not offset the losses from reduced business and international segments.

evidence.text: International passenger revenue decreased 21.7% year-over-year due to decreased demand as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.
The reductions in revenue and capacity discussed below were a result of reduced demand and government travel directives limiting or suspending air travel due to the spread of COVID-19.
The current reduction in demand for air travel due to the COVID-19 pandemic has resulted in an unprecedented low level of advance bookings and the associated cash received.

The decrease in passenger commissions and other selling expenses is primarily related to the significant reduction in demand for travel beginning in March 2020 due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Shifts in travel demand are unfavorable, with significant declines in international and business travel, affecting yields and margins.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is negatively shifting due to reduced demand in both business and leisure travel, driven by the COVID-19 pandemic. Additionally, the shift is toward lower-margin segments, further impacting the company's financial performance.

evidence.text: These effects related to the COVID-19 pandemic are negatively impacting air travel in general, which in turn are negatively affecting our revenues and results of operations.

At this time we are also not able to predict whether the COVID-19 pandemic will result in permanent changes to our customers' behavior, with such changes including but not limited to a permanent reduction in business travel as a result of increased usage of 'virtual' and 'teleconferencing' products and more broadly a general reluctance to travel by consumers, each of which could have a material impact on our business.

The decrease in passenger commissions and other selling expenses is primarily related to the significant reduction in demand for travel beginning in March 2020 due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is negatively shifting due to reduced demand in both business and leisure travel, impacting financial performance.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its debt leverage due to new debt issuances and borrowings to manage through the challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: We have a significant amount of existing fixed obligations [...] through the issuance of additional debt securities as well as through bilateral and syndicated secured and/or unsecured credit facilities.

In March 2020, we entered into a $2.7 billion 364-day secured term loan facility [...] In April 2020, this loan was increased to $3.0 billion. [...] We drew $3.0 billion from our previously undrawn revolving credit facilities.

We have agreed to acquire four A350 aircraft from LATAM and assumed ten of LATAM's A350 purchase commitments from Airbus, with deliveries through 2025, which are included as purchase commitments in the table above.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased debt leverage is a headwind due to new debt issuances, though it provides liquidity during the pandemic.

factors.summary: The backlog of orders is showing mixed trends. While the total value of refunds increased, indicating a decrease in backlog orders due to flight cancellations and reduced demand, the backlog itself is stable as new orders are not outpacing revenue.

evidence.text: The total value of refunds, excluding taxes and related fees, issued to customers during the March 2020 quarter was approximately $850 million.

We expect to meet our cash needs for the next twelve months with cash and cash equivalents, financing arrangements, government assistance from the CARES Act, restricted cash equivalents and cash flows from operations.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Backlog orders show mixed trends with decreased refunds but stable backlog, indicating no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rates on the company is mixed. While lower interest rates have reduced interest expense, there are risks related to potential breaches of financial covenants due to reduced earnings from the COVID-19 pandemic. Additionally, new low-interest loans under the CARES Act have affected interest rates, providing some financial relief but introducing complexities in the final years of the loan term.

evidence.text: Our primary credit facility has various financial and other covenants that require us to maintain a minimum fixed charge coverage ratio and a minimum asset coverage ratio. Based on the reduction in demand that we are currently experiencing as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic and given the limited visibility to the future recovery of demand, there is a range of possible outcomes where our earnings could be reduced enough to result in a breach of the minimum fixed charge coverage ratio within the next year.
evidence.subsection: Agreements governing our debt, including credit agreements, include financial and other covenants. Failure to comply with these covenants could result in events of default.

evidence.text: The loan includes annual interest rates of 1.00% for the first five years (through April 2025) and the Secured Overnight Financing Rate ('SOFR') plus 2.00% in the final five years.
Interest expense decreased compared to the prior year period as a result of lower interest rates on our debt.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The impact of interest rates is mixed, with lower expenses but risks of covenant breaches, providing a balanced view.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure and compressing due to decreased demand, increased expenses related to COVID-19, and reduced pricing power.

evidence.text: We expect fuel consumption to decline consistent with the capacity reductions we are making in response to the pandemic.
Total operating expense decreased by $450 million, but the decrease in revenue due to lower demand resulted in a significant compression of margins.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under significant pressure due to decreased demand, increased expenses, and reduced pricing power.

factors.summary: Organic growth is expected to be modest, with a deceleration in revenue growth due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. The outlook for the remainder of the year is unclear, but a modest demand recovery is anticipated to begin in the September 2020 quarter.

evidence.text: The outlook for the remainder of the year is unclear, but we are currently planning for a modest demand recovery beginning in the September 2020 quarter.

We expect to meet our cash needs for the next twelve months with cash and cash equivalents, financing arrangements, government assistance from the CARES Act, restricted cash equivalents and cash flows from operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is expected to be modest with a deceleration in revenue growth due to the pandemic.

factors.summary: Fuel expenses are a significant component of operating costs, with notable volatility in market prices. Despite this volatility, there is an expectation of a decline in fuel expenses due to both lower market prices and reduced consumption, driven by capacity reductions in response to the pandemic.

evidence.text: Fuel expense represented approximately 18% of our total operating expenses for the three months ended March 31, 2020. The market price for jet fuel is volatile, which can impact the comparability of our periodic cash flows from operations. We expect fuel consumption to decline consistent with the capacity reductions we are making in response to the pandemic.
Fuel expense decreased $383 million compared to the prior year quarter primarily due to an approximately 8% decrease in the market price per gallon of jet fuel and a 8% decrease in consumption.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Decline in fuel expenses due to lower prices and reduced consumption.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed through various measures, including salary reductions, furloughs, and a hiring freeze, but are also impacted by CARES Act restrictions on layoffs and furloughs.

evidence.text: The relief payments are conditioned on our agreement to refrain from conducting involuntary employee layoffs or furloughs through September 30, 2020.

As a result of decreased demand for air travel due to the COVID-19 pandemic, we have instituted a hiring freeze, reduced salaries by 50% and 25% for our officer and director level employees, respectively, and reduced work hours by 25% for all other management and most front-line employee work groups for the June 2020 quarter.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Costs are being managed through various measures, but CARES Act restrictions limit actions.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns, including dividends and buybacks, have been suspended due to the pandemic.

evidence.text: In early March 2020, we suspended both our share repurchase program and future dividends due to the impact of the pandemic.
Suspending future share repurchases and dividends.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns have been suspended, indicating financial stress.

factors.summary: The company is implementing various cost reduction actions, including delaying pension contributions, deferring capital expenditures, reducing salaries, and parking aircraft to conserve liquidity and manage expenses during challenging conditions.

evidence.text: We have no minimum funding requirements in 2020. As part of our liquidity initiatives, we are delaying $500 million of voluntary pension funding that we were previously planning for 2020. [...] We are deferring substantially all of our previously planned 2020 capital expenditures.
We are parking over 650 mainline and regional aircraft and expect to remove from active service over 650 mainline and regional aircraft until demand recovers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Implementing various cost reduction actions to conserve liquidity.

factors.summary: Free cash flow has significantly deteriorated compared to the prior year, driven by reduced revenue and increased financing activities.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $358 million compared to $1,942 million in the prior year.
Total free cash flow was $(161) million compared to $751 million in the prior year.

We expect to meet our cash needs for the next twelve months with cash and cash equivalents, financing arrangements, government assistance from the CARES Act, restricted cash equivalents and cash flows from operations.

sentiment.label: very_negative

factors.summary: The company has significantly reduced its capital expenditures due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, reflecting a cautious approach to future growth. This reduction is evident in both the immediate actions taken, such as limiting non-critical investments, and the substantial cuts in planned capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Our expected 2020 investments total $1.2 billion, of which $937 million was in the March quarter and substantially all occurred before the steps taken to mitigate the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. Planned investments for the remainder of the year are limited to those critical to our operation.

As a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, we have taken, and are continuing to take, certain actions to increase liquidity and strengthen our financial position which include reducing planned capital expenditures by approximately $3.5 billion.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significantly reduced capital expenditures due to the pandemic.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include challenges related to GOL's ability to repay its term loan due to the pandemic, as well as reduced demand for air travel and potential economic downturns.

evidence.text: As the COVID-19 pandemic continues to impact the global economy, there is an increased risk related to GOL's ability to repay this term loan, which may require our performance under this guarantee.
The COVID-19 pandemic is causing a significant decrease in demand for air travel, leading to substantial reductions in revenue and operational adjustments.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include challenges related to repaying term loans and reduced demand.

factors.summary: Fuel costs are experiencing volatility, with a noted decrease in average prices and a favorable hedging impact. The company is managing this risk through expected declines in consumption.

evidence.text: The market price for jet fuel is volatile, which can impact the comparability of our periodic cash flows from operations. We expect fuel consumption to decline consistent with the capacity reductions we are making in response to the pandemic.
The average price per gallon decreased from $2.06 to $1.81, and the hedging impact resulted in a slight decrease in fuel expense.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Fuel costs have decreased and hedging has slightly reduced expenses, but the volatility and expected declines in consumption present mixed signals.

factors.summary: The company is adjusting its route network to reduce capacity and focus on critical operations, potentially exiting unprofitable routes, and optimizing aircraft deliveries in response to decreased demand due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Planned investments for the remainder of the year are limited to those critical to our operation.

To align capacity with customer demand as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, we are working with OEMs to optimize the timing of our future aircraft deliveries and expect to remove from active service over 650 mainline and regional aircraft until demand recovers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Adjusting route networks to reduce capacity and exit unprofitable routes indicates operational challenges and reduced demand.

factors.summary: The company's fleet strategy focuses on reducing financial strain by deferring non-critical aircraft purchases, optimizing delivery timings, and retiring older aircraft. These measures are intended to conserve resources during periods of low demand.

evidence.text: We are working with the OEMs to optimize the timing of our future aircraft deliveries. [...] We are deferring substantially all of our previously planned 2020 capital expenditures.
We are working with OEMs to optimize the timing of our future aircraft deliveries and expect to remove from active service over 650 mainline and regional aircraft until demand recovers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Deferring purchases and retiring aircraft suggests financial strain and reduced future capacity.

factors.summary: The company has potential exposure related to guarantees of GOL's term loan, particularly due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on GOL's ability to repay the loan. However, there is no explicit mention of litigation or investigations affecting the company in the operating expense section.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly new tariffs on aircraft imports and emissions-related requirements for refineries, are impacting the company's operations. These changes may affect future deliveries and impose additional operational and financial burdens.

evidence.text: In October 2019, the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative announced a 10% tariff on new aircraft imported from Europe, which was subsequently increased to 15% in February 2020. We are continuing to evaluate the impact of this announcement on our future Airbus deliveries.

A refinery is subject to annual U.S. Environmental Protection Agency requirements to blend renewable fuels into the gasoline and on-road diesel fuel it produces. Alternatively, a refinery may purchase renewable energy credits, called Renewable Identification Numbers ('RINs'), from third parties in the secondary market.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: New tariffs and emissions requirements increase operational and financial burdens.

factors.summary: The company faces potential geopolitical risks, particularly from trade tensions impacting aircraft imports. While the Sources of Liquidity subsection highlights specific trade tariffs imposed on European aircraft, the Operating Expense subsection does not address geopolitical risks as a significant operational factor.

evidence.text: In October 2019, the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative announced a 10% tariff on new aircraft imported from Europe, which was subsequently increased to 15% in February 2020.
No specific mention of geopolitical risks affecting the company's operations.

sentiment.label: neutral

factors.summary: The company's foreign exchange exposure is mixed, with potential risks from equity investments impacting liquidity but not significantly affecting results due to cost reduction and liquidity management efforts.

evidence.text: Unrealized gains/losses on our equity investments in GOL, China Eastern, Air France-KLM and Hanjin-KAL [...] are driven by changes in stock prices and foreign currency.

Miscellaneous, net is primarily composed of our proportionate share of earnings/losses from our equity investments in Virgin Atlantic and Grupo Aeroméxico, pension and related expense, charitable contributions and foreign exchange gains/losses. Our equity investment financial results and foreign exchange gains/losses vary and impact the comparability of miscellaneous, net from period to period.

sentiment.label: neutral
